purpose: Separate evidence retrieval from verified claim support before training a citation verifier.

candidate_evidence.definition: The span is relevant enough to inspect, but support has not been established.

verified_support.definition: The span directly supports the claim under point-in-time constraints.

partial_support.definition: The span supports only part of a composite claim or supports a weaker version of it.

insufficient.definition: The span is topical or adjacent but does not provide enough evidence for the claim.

contradicts.definition: The span conflicts with or materially weakens the claim.

guardrails: A headline can be candidate_evidence but is rarely verified_support for a financial thesis.
A social post is opinion evidence unless backed by official or auditable source spans.
Composite claims require each subclaim to be supported or the label becomes partial_support.
Any source published after as_of is temporal_leakage and cannot be verified_support.
Do not train final-answer citation correctness from candidate_evidence rows without relabeling.
